expectations.text: Each issue lists specific files or directories it will modify (not vague references like 'the API layer')
expectations.evidence: Every issue lists specific file paths like 'src/server/api/routes/widget.ts', 'src/server/services/embed-renderer.ts', 'src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/widgets/[id]/page.tsx'. A full file footprint matrix is also provided.

expectations.text: Output includes a parallel lanes or concurrency view showing which issues can run simultaneously
expectations.evidence: Includes explicit 'Parallel lanes view' section with 4 lanes and a 7-phase execution timeline showing which issues run in parallel at each phase.

expectations.text: No two issues that are simultaneously unblocked (same lane position, no dependency between them) share the same files
expectations.evidence: Phase 6 runs #8 (page.tsx) and #7 (page.tsx) in parallel. Phase 7 runs #9 (page.tsx) and #10 (page.tsx + embed-renderer.ts) in parallel. The output acknowledges this: 'there is a small risk of merge conflict on the file level' and offers both conservative (serialize) and aggressive approaches, but the lanes view shows the aggressive approach.

expectations.text: Issues that touch shared files (e.g., widgetConfigSchema, embed-renderer.ts) are chained via dependencies rather than left independent
expectations.evidence: #1 and #2 are serialized for widget.ts/embed-renderer.ts/widgetin.ts overlap. #3 depends on #1, #4 depends on #2 for embed-renderer.ts. Editor issues (#5-#11) are chained to avoid page.tsx conflicts. Full dependency graph and conflict resolution rationale provided.

expectations.text: File paths referenced in the breakdown correspond to actual files in the widgetin codebase
expectations.evidence: All paths (src/server/api/routes/widget.ts, src/server/services/embed-renderer.ts, src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/widgets/[id]/page.tsx, src/server/db/schema/widgetin.ts, etc.) correspond to real files in the repo.

expectations.text: Issues are vertical slices (end-to-end), not horizontal layers (e.g., not 'all schema changes' as one issue)
expectations.evidence: Issues are organized as horizontal layers: #1-#2 are schema-only, #3-#4 are renderer-only, #5-#9 are editor-only. This is deliberate (to minimize file conflicts) but contradicts the vertical slice requirement. E.g., Issue #1 'Add granular card styling to config schema' only touches the schema/interface layers, not the renderer or editor.

suggestions.assertion: Issues are vertical slices (end-to-end), not horizontal layers
suggestions.reason: There is a fundamental tension between vertical slices and conflict avoidance. Vertical slices guarantee each issue touches schema+renderer+editor (3 shared files), making conflict-free parallelism nearly impossible. The skill should address this tension explicitly — perhaps by keeping vertical slices but using dependencies to chain them, rather than splitting into horizontal layers.

eval_feedback.overall: The with-skill output is dramatically better at conflict analysis but traded vertical slices for horizontal layers to achieve it. The skill instructions need to reconcile this tension.
